        # Narration script — Module 8: UVM Miscellaneous Utilities

        **Target length:** ~103 minutes (207 slides; auto-generated — edit per slide as needed)

        ## Timing table

        | Slide | Section | Duration | Narration |
|-------|---------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | Module 8 | 0:25 | Welcome to module 8, UVM Miscellaneous Utilities. In this module you will master uvm utility classes. |
| 2 | Learning objectives | 0:16 | Here is what you will learn in this module. Master UVM utility classes |
| 3 | Prerequisites | 0:16 | Before you start, make sure you have these prerequisites. See module README |
| 4 | Learning path | 0:22 | Learning path. Master UVM utility classes |
| 5 | Overview | 0:16 | Overview. This module covers UVM utility classes including Command Line Processor, comparators, recorders, pools, queues, and string/math/random... |
| 6 | Design architecture | 0:08 | Next section: Design architecture. |
| 7 | 1. UVM utility layer architecture (1/3) | 0:38 | 1. UVM utility layer architecture (1/3). Utilities sit below agents/env — used by transactions, scoreboards, tests CLP is singleton — query in build_phase or test new() for test configuration Comparators plug into do_compare for field-tolerant matching Integration example (examples/integration/) wires multiple utilities in one TB Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 8 | 1. UVM utility layer architecture (2/3) | 0:28 | 1. UVM utility layer architecture (2/3). uvm_cmdline_processor: Parses +UVM_* and custom plusargs at startup uvm_comparer / uvm_packer: Transaction compare and pack/unpack utilities uvm_recorder: Transaction recording for debug and replay uvm_pool / queues: Shared storage across components |
| 9 | 1. UVM utility layer architecture (3/3) | 0:16 | 1. UVM utility layer architecture (3/3). String/math/random utils: Formatting, statistics, and RNG helpers |
| 10 | 2. Utility integration flow (1/2) | 0:38 | 2. Utility integration flow (1/2). 1: CLP — Read +TESTNAME=, +UVM_VERBOSITY=, seed plusargs 2: Random utils — Seed RNG for repeatable constrained-random runs 3: Transaction — pack/unpack for scoreboard queues or recording 4: Recorder — Log high-value transactions to file for offline debug Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 11 | 2. Utility integration flow (2/2) | 0:16 | 2. Utility integration flow (2/2). 5: Report — Summarize comparer mismatches and pool statistics |
| 12 | 3. Module 8 example catalog | 0:34 | 3. Module 8 example catalog. One example per utility family (CLP, comparators, recorders, pools, queues, string/math/random) dut/dma/simple_dma.v provides lightweight RTL context for integration demo test_utilities_uvm.sv shows utilities inside a full UVM test structure Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 13 | RTL block diagram (reference) | 0:22 | RTL block diagram (reference). Module 8: DUT hierarchy and signal flow. |
| 14 | Verification / testbench diagram (reference) | 0:22 | Verification / testbench diagram (reference). Module 8: stimulus, observation, and checking. |
| 15 | Execution & simulation flow | 0:08 | Next section: Execution & simulation flow. |
| 16 | How the example runs (toolchain) | 0:32 | How the example runs (toolchain). Match each bullet to files in the repository. Makefile: Verilator + UVM_HOME compile RTL, interface, and test_*.sv UVM phases: build → connect → run_test → report (same pattern as Module 2) Sequence → driver → DUT → monitor → scoreboard (read SCOREBOARD at end) Typical command: make SIM=verilator TEST=test_* (see module8 demo slide) Loopback / hooks connect... |
| 17 | Artifact flow (spec → sim) (1) | 0:28 | Artifact flow (spec → sim) (1). Match each bullet to files in the repository. 1: CLP — Read +TESTNAME=, +UVM_VERBOSITY=, seed plusargs 2: Random utils — Seed RNG for repeatable constrained-random runs 3: Transaction — pack/unpack for scoreboard queues or recording 4: Recorder — Log high-value transactions to file for offline debug From MODULE8 — execution and artifact layout. |
| 18 | Artifact flow (spec → sim) (2) | 0:16 | Artifact flow (spec → sim) (2). Match each bullet to files in the repository. 5: Report — Summarize comparer mismatches and pool statistics From MODULE8 — execution and artifact layout. |
| 19 | Example directory layout | 0:32 | Example directory layout. Match each bullet to files in the repository. uvm_cmdline_processor: Parses +UVM_* and custom plusargs at startup uvm_comparer / uvm_packer: Transaction compare and pack/unpack utilities uvm_recorder: Transaction recording for debug and replay uvm_pool / queues: Shared storage across components String/math/random utils: Formatting, statistics, and RNG helpers Match... |
| 20 | Directed test execution sequence | 0:28 | Follow these steps in order when working through this module. parse args configure test run utility-backed report Follow this order when tracing waveforms or debugging. |
| 21 | Verification & testing methods | 0:08 | Next section: Verification & testing methods. |
| 22 | 1. Utility-driven test configuration | 0:38 | 1. Utility-driven test configuration. Use +UVM_TESTNAME= and +UVM_VERBOSITY= instead of hard-coded test selection Pass custom plusargs (+NUM_TRANS=, +TIMEOUT=) via CLP getters Seed control: plusarg → uvm_random_utils or $urandom for reproducible bugs Recorders capture failing transaction windows for post-sim review Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 23 | 2. Debug and regression strategy (1/2) | 0:38 | 2. Debug and regression strategy (1/2). Enable comparer policy (ignore certain fields) for noisy buses Pools share counters/statistics between monitor and scoreboard without globals Regression: ./scripts/module8.sh --all-examples --uvm-tests ./scripts/module8.sh --integration validates combined utility usage Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 24 | 2. Debug and regression strategy (2/2) | 0:16 | 2. Debug and regression strategy (2/2). Closure: utility self-tests PASS + integration test UVM report clean |

| 79 | Detail: Type Casting (1) | 0:28 | Protocol and design detail: Type Casting (1). real'(int_value): Convert integer to real int'(real_value): Convert real to integer Too Small: Frequent object creation/deletion overhead Too Large: Unnecessary memory usage From MODULE8 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |
| 80 | Detail: Type Casting (2) | 0:28 | Protocol and design detail: Type Casting (2). Optimal: Match typical allocation pattern (e.g., 2x average concurrent objects) FIFO Queue: Use size() check before pop_front() to avoid warnings Priority Queue: insert() has O(n) complexity - consider heap for large queues Associative Arrays: foreach iteration - cache results if frequently accessed From MODULE8 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |

| 156 | 9. Integration (23/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (23/31). Too Large: Unnecessary memory usage Optimal: Match typical allocation pattern (e.g., 2x average concurrent objects) FIFO Queue: Use size() check before pop_front() to avoid warnings Priority Queue: insert() has O(n) complexity - consider heap for large queues Associative Arrays: foreach iteration - cache results if frequently accessed |
| 157 | 9. Integration (24/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (24/31). Substring Extraction: substr() creates new string - cache if reused Character Access: getc() is O(1) - prefer over substring for single characters In-Order: O(1) per transaction if queues balanced Out-of-Order: O(n) per transaction for foreach iteration Optimization: Use num() to check map size before iteration |
| 158 | 9. Integration (25/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (25/31). Consideration: Limit recording to relevant transactions or use sampling Problem: Configuration uses default values Solution: Check return value, provide defaults Code: if (uvm_cmdline_proc.get_arg_value("+arg", str_val)) value = sv_atoi(str_val); Problem: Transactions never arrive in both queues |
| 159 | 9. Integration (26/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (26/31). Code: Verify expected_port.connect() and actual_port.connect() Problem: Pool runs out of objects Solution: Increase pool size or check free() calls Code: pool_size = 20; or ensure all objects are freed Problem: Transactions not in priority order |
| 160 | 9. Integration (27/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (27/31). Code: if (prio > rhs.prio) return 1; (higher priority = higher value) Problem: Field not extracted correctly Solution: Check string format and field name Code: Verify format matches expected pattern Problem: Average calculation truncates |
| 161 | 9. Integration (28/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (28/31). Code: return sum / real'(values.size()); Problem: $urandom_range() returns wrong values Solution: Parameters are max, min (not min, max) Code: $urandom_range(max_val, min_val) not $urandom_range(min_val, max_val) Check: Plusarg format (+name=value) |
| 162 | 9. Integration (29/31) | 0:36 | 9. Integration (29/31). Check: Check return value (1 = found, 0 = not found) Debug: Print all plusargs using get_plusargs() Solution: Verify plusargs passed correctly on command line Check: Analysis port connections Check: Both write_expected() and write_actual() called |

| 194 | Common pitfalls | 0:08 | Next section: Common pitfalls. |
| 195 | CLP Plusarg Not Found | 0:24 | CLP Plusarg Not Found. Problem: Configuration uses default values Solution: Check return value, provide defaults Code: if (uvm_cmdline_proc.get_arg_value("+arg", str_val)) value = sv_atoi(str_val); |
| 196 | Comparator Queues Never Match | 0:24 | Comparator Queues Never Match. Problem: Transactions never arrive in both queues Solution: Check analysis port connections Code: Verify expected_port.connect() and actual_port.connect() |
| 197 | Pool Exhaustion | 0:24 | Pool Exhaustion. Problem: Pool runs out of objects Solution: Increase pool size or check free() calls Code: pool_size = 20; or ensure all objects are freed |
| 198 | Priority Queue Wrong Order | 0:24 | Priority Queue Wrong Order. Problem: Transactions not in priority order Solution: Check compare() method implementation Code: if (prio > rhs.prio) return 1; (higher priority = higher value) |
| 199 | String Extraction Fails | 0:24 | String Extraction Fails. Problem: Field not extracted correctly Solution: Check string format and field name Code: Verify format matches expected pattern |
| 200 | Math Division Truncation | 0:24 | Math Division Truncation. Problem: Average calculation truncates Solution: Use real'() cast for floating-point division Code: return sum / real'(values.size()); |

        ## Section narration (edit for TTS)

        - **Design architecture (UVM utility layer architecture, Utility integration flow, Module 8 example catalog):** Walk through the block diagram, then relate each block to files under module8/examples/.
- **Execution:** Explain make run / UVM make steps, then walk the artifact table and directed-test sequence slide by slide.
- **Verification (Utility-driven test configuration, Debug and regression strategy):** Explain what stimulus is applied, what is checked, and what is intentionally out of scope.
- **Syllabus:** Cover 9 topic section(s) — pause on protocol timing and signals.
- **Before exercises:** Ask learners to recall the learning outcomes slide; they should explain each bullet in their own words.
- **Hands-on:** Run module8/EXAMPLES.md labs; narrate expected PASS lines.

        ## Notes

        - Slides from **Before You Start**, **Design Architecture**, **Verification & Testing Methods**, **Topics Covered**, **EXAMPLES.md**, and **Learning Outcomes**.
        - Full detail: `docs/MODULE8.md` and `module8/EXAMPLES.md`.
        - Regenerate: `regenerate_course_outlines.sh <course_root> --module 8`
